canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, MIRACL is consistently characterized as a dataset for evaluating retrieval systems, with a specific focus on multilingual contexts. It is identified as the "Multilingual Information Retrieval Across a Continuum of Languages" dataset, designed for evaluation across 18 diverse languages. Papers use MIRACL to measure both general "Information retrieval" and, more specifically, "Multilingual ability." One study explicitly states its purpose is "to assess... multilingual retrieval capability." The dataset is described as containing "native questions" and "relevance judgements of retrieved passages" to facilitate this evaluation. In addition to direct use, MIRACL also serves as a foundation for new evaluation tools, as one paper notes its own benchmark "builds on the popular MIRACL dataset."
papers: BoostingLLM’s Molecular Structure Elucidation with Knowledge Enhanced Tree Search Reasoning
VISA: Retrieval Augmented Generation with Visual Source Attribution
MMTEB: Massive Multilingual Text Embedding Benchmark
NV-Embed: Improved Techniques for Training LLMs as Generalist Embedding Models

definitions.text: The Multilingual Information Retrieval Across a Continuum of Languages dataset, used for evaluating retrieval systems across 18 diverse languages.
definitions.paper: NV-Embed: Improved Techniques for Training LLMs as Generalist Embedding Models

relationships.paper: VISA: Retrieval Augmented Generation with Visual Source Attribution
relationships.evidence: Our main evaluations are conducted on BEIR (Thakur et al., 2021) and MIRACL (Zhang et al., 2023), to assess the generalization of dense retrievers and multilingual retrieval capability. (Section 4.5).
